methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

data.narrative:
Nimble Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company currently valued at $1.0B post-money, having raised $221M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage proximity, including Hadrian, 1X Technologies, Exotec, and Dexterity. The peer cohort exhibits substantial dispersion, ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the last post-money valuation of $1.0B, implying $1.7B at the next financing stage. Peer-median analysis across the 8-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with the range spanning $300M to $10.0B. These methods triangulate to a median implied value of $1.7B, with a low-end estimate of $1.4B and a high-end estimate of $2.0B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.0B, anchored at $1.7B. Key uncertainties include the substantial spread within the peer set—driven largely by differences in stage maturity and market positioning—and the limited public market comparables (n=1), which constrains confidence in market-multiple approaches. The tight clustering of stage-progression and peer-median results provides modest additional support for the triangulated range.
